article_title: CPUID Website Hijacked to Distribute STX RAT Through Trojanized CPU-Z and HWMonitor Downloads

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: CPUID Website Hijacked to Distribute STX RAT Through Trojanized CPU-Z and HWMonitor Downloads
content_preview.summary: Attackers compromised CPUID's backend API and replaced download links for four popular hardware tools with malware-laden installers, infecting over 150 users across multiple countries.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The official website of CPUID, the French software vendor behind the widely used hardware diagnostic tools CPU-Z and HWMonitor, was compromised between April 9 and April 10, 2026, with attackers replacing legitimate download links with trojanized installers carrying the STX remote access trojan. Kaspersky, which led the public analysis, has identified more than 150 confirmed infections so far, according to [BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/supply-chai...

editor_notes.content_quality: Solid cybersecurity article covering CPUID supply-chain breach distributing STX RAT via CPU-Z/HWMonitor.
editor_notes.source_verification: The Register VERIFIED via WebFetch: April 9-10 breach, ~6h secondary API compromise, HWMonitor 64-bit targeted, PowerShell in-memory, Chrome credential access all confirmed. BleepingComputer and Hacker News snapshots captured.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Breach timeline, affected tools (CPU-Z/HWMonitor), STX RAT characteristics, API compromise vector all verified.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral technical tone. Covers incident without sensationalism.
editor_notes.originality: No prior CPUID or STX RAT coverage.
